static_findings.description:
Reentrancy in TimestampVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#70-89):
	External calls:
	- token.transfer(beneficiary,releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#85)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- TokensReleased(ids[i],releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#86)

Reentrancy in TimestampVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#52-68):
	External calls:
	- token.transfer(g.beneficiary,releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#66)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- TokensReleased(grantId,releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#67)

Reentrancy in TimestampVesting.createGrant(address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#43-50):
	External calls:
	- token.transferFrom(msg.sender,address(this),totalAmount) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#47)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- GrantCreated(id,beneficiary,totalAmount) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#48)

Reentrancy in TimestampVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#70-89):
	External calls:
	- token.transfer(beneficiary,releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#85)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- g.released += releasable (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#84)
	TimestampVesting.grants (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#27) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- TimestampVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#70-89)
	- TimestampVesting.createGrant(address,uint256,uint256,uint256,bool) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#43-50)
	- TimestampVesting.getVestedAmount(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#99-105)
	- TimestampVesting.grants (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#27)
	- TimestampVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#52-68)
	- TimestampVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#91-97)

TimestampVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#91-97) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(g.revocable,not revocable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#93)

TimestampVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#70-89) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- elapsed < g.cliffDuration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#76)
	- elapsed >= g.vestingDuration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#78)
	- releasable > 0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#83)

TimestampVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#52-68) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(msg.sender == g.beneficiary || msg.sender == owner,not authorized) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#54)
	- elapsed < g.cliffDuration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#57)
	- elapsed >= g.vestingDuration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#59)
	- require(bool,string)(releasable > 0,nothing to release) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#64)

TimestampVesting.getVestedAmount(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#99-105) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- elapsed < g.cliffDuration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#102)
	- elapsed >= g.vestingDuration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_5bfg9fle.sol#103)
Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.
Invoking `SSTORE` operations in loops may waste gas. Use a local variable to hold the loop computation result.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
Check for `address(0)` when assigning values to address state variables.
State variables that are only changed in the constructor should be declared immutable to save gas. Add the `immutable` attribute to state variables that are only changed in the constructor
Function returns a value but it is ignored. Consider checking the return value.
ERC20 functions may not behave as expected. For example: return values are not always meaningful. It is recommended to use OpenZeppelin's SafeERC20 library.
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`

final_report.recommendation:
HIGH RISK — top vulnerability: Timestamp (probability 91.3%, CONFIRMED tier). 0 similar exploit pattern(s) found in DeFiHackLabs corpus. 2 Slither High/Medium finding(s). 2 prior on-chain audit(s). Recommend full manual audit before deployment.

> **NOTE:** Contract exceeded 512 CodeBERT tokens — tail code was not analysed. Manual review of the unanalysed portion is recommended.

unused_evidence: 1 High/Medium static finding(s) not tied to a verdict: reentrancy-state-change
failure_modes: Contract exceeded 512 CodeBERT tokens — tail code unanalysed; verdicts may miss vulnerabilities in the truncated region.
ExternalBug flagged — the ML model is known to over-predict this class (Run 12 class-definition mismatch); treat as lower confidence unless corroborated by an inter-contract call finding.
reflection_notes.summary: Self-critique: 8 verdict(s) are uncertain; 1 evidence item(s) unused; 2 failure mode(s) noted.
